title: crinum latifolium var. zeylanicum
intro: Crinum latifolium var. zeylanicum, also known as the Ceylon Crinum or Wild Gloriosa, is a plant species native to Sri Lanka, belonging to the Amaryllidaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Crinum latifolium var. zeylanicum include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the plant's bulbs and leaves are used to make infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, which can be used to treat various ailments.
intro_study: According to "Immunopharmacology", Crinum latifolium var. zeylanicum (which is likely a variation of Crinum asiaticum used in the study) may possess anti-inflammatory and anti-histaminic properties, reducing local pain and inflammation.
